title: >= 250 educator-reviewed alignments across >= 2 subjects
context: OER are abundant and free but are not indexed against the curriculum standards that govern what teachers must teach, so teachers cannot find the free resource aligned to a given standard. This project builds an open crosswalk of educator-reviewed AlignmentRecords linking OER to standards by stable identifier (CASE GUID / ASN URI), not by reproducing copyrighted standards text. Two hard gates never skip: the dual licence/terms gate (gate-002) and educator review of every record, with a blind second-reviewer precision audit holding precision at >= 95%. Scaling the reviewed crosswalk across two subjects and delivering it to the first confirmed consumer.
objective: Produce >= 250 educator-reviewed alignments across >= 2 subjects, delivered to the confirmed consumer with the coverage ledger updated.
acceptanceCriteria: Every record passed gate-002 and educator review (accepted), with evidence + strength + coverage recorded; the AI score is never the ship signal.
Every CASE GUID / ASN URI resolves (resolver green); records schema-valid; exports validate.
Provenance complete both sides; reference-by-identifier respected; reproduction only where permitted.
Delivered to / published for the confirmed consumer (partner-016) with the Steward's delivery-evidence artifact; coverage ledger updated.
